codeReview.summary: Fail. The commit adds substantial cross-area Jest coverage, but three claimed assertions are still under-verified: the no-secret-in-logs test never executes the unlock/create/restore phases it claims to scan, the crash-reporter negative check only inspects package.json and src/ while skipping ios/ and android/, and the __DEV__ redaction test asserts a hypothetical local serializer instead of any real inspector/devtools snapshot path.

issues.description: The VAL-CROSS-011 test does not actually cover the full lifecycle it claims to scan for log leaks. It calls initializeFirstLaunch(), renders RecoveryPhrase/RecoveryRestore, then tears down and locks the stores, but it never executes createIdentity(), unlockAgent(), or restoreFromMnemonic(). Any mnemonic/secret logging introduced in those required phases would be missed even though the feature claims full-flow coverage.

The VAL-CROSS-012 negative check only scans package.json and src/ for sentry|bugsnag|crashlytics. The feature description and validation contract require checking whether no crash reporter SDK is wired anywhere, including ios/ and android/. Native-only crash reporter wiring would evade this test completely.

The VAL-CROSS-013 test does not exercise any real devtools/logger serialization path. It defines a local safeSerialize() helper that always redacts recoveryPhrase and then asserts that helper's output, so the test would still pass even if an actual __DEV__ inspector snapshot serialized the raw mnemonic. This does not verify the behavior the assertion is supposed to pin.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true, but the transcript skeleton does not show the required context reads for this feature. The mobile-feature-worker skill requires reading mission.md plus the relevant library docs (architecture.md at minimum, and environment.md/user-testing.md when running tests and asserting user-facing anchors), yet this session only shows reads of AGENTS.md, validation-contract.md, services.yaml, and app source files before implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:17-28 requires mission.md, architecture.md, environment.md, and user-testing.md in this scenario; the workerSessionId 1494b9a0-7072-405a-bfd8-20ff1df93f1d transcript skeleton shows no reads of those files before test creation; handoffs/2026-04-22T22-48-06-818Z__cross-area-integration-tests__1494b9a0-7072-405a-bfd8-20ff1df93f1d.json:132-135 reports followedProcedure=true with deviations=[]

summary: Reviewed commit cae6697f4387610882df3dc9222b6afc104b8da9 for cross-area-integration-tests. The suite adds meaningful cross-area coverage, but it does not fully verify three claimed contract points (full-flow log scanning, crash-reporter absence across native code, and real __DEV__ snapshot redaction), so this review fails with three blocking test-coverage defects. Logged one shared-state observation about inaccurate procedure compliance in the worker handoff.
